meta.scene_summary: We are building the future with one hand while the other hand is desperately trying to unplug our own smart TVs.

cooking.headline: YOLO26 just dropped to make sure your webcam never misses a beat
cooking.summary: Ultralytics released another vision model, which is great news if you enjoy updating your dependencies every time you blink. It is the tech equivalent of a software update that promises to see everything, assuming you can actually get it to compile.
cooking.plain_english: Ultralytics has released a new version of their real-time computer vision model designed to improve performance and integration.

cooking.headline: A 3B model is body-slamming the giants because apparently size isn't everything
cooking.summary: VibeThinker is out here proving you don't need a supercomputer to outsmart the heavyweights if your training math is actually good. It's the AI equivalent of the scrawny kid in the gym who somehow benches more than the entire varsity team.
cooking.plain_english: A small artificial intelligence model with three billion parameters has achieved performance results superior to larger models using new training techniques.

cooking.headline: Canada is speedrunning a nuclear renaissance to keep the lights on
cooking.summary: Canada decided that building ten nuclear reactors is the only way to satisfy our collective hunger for more compute and air conditioning. It's a bold move to trade long-term grid stability for the ability to keep training models without blowing a fuse.
cooking.plain_english: The Canadian government has announced a strategic plan to build up to ten new nuclear reactors by 2040 to increase power generation capacity.

cooked.headline: Linux Secure Boot certificates are expiring like a forgotten grocery item
cooked.summary: The security certificates that keep your boot process locked down are hitting their expiration date, creating a massive headache for distro maintainers. It turns out that digital security is a lot like a fridge; if you don't check the dates, you're going to have a very bad time.
cooked.plain_english: The digital security certificates used to verify the Linux operating system during startup are expiring, which requires updates to prevent system errors.

cooked.headline: Meta's AI training program was caught stalking its own employees
cooked.summary: Meta was using an internal tool to track employee keystrokes to train AI, only to have the data leak and expose the whole operation. Nothing says 'innovation' quite like accidentally turning your office into a surveillance state for the sake of a chatbot.
cooked.plain_english: Meta paused a project that recorded employee activity, including keystrokes, to train artificial intelligence after the data was exposed.

cooked.headline: Oracle is shedding 20,000 employees to optimize for 'efficiency'
cooked.summary: Oracle cut 20,000 roles this year, proving that even a tech giant treats its workforce like a legacy codebase that needs refactoring. It's the corporate equivalent of deleting your entire 'node_modules' folder and hoping the project still compiles.
cooked.plain_english: Oracle has reduced its global workforce by approximately 20,000 employees over the past year.

cooked.headline: SpaceX stock just hit a gravity-defying low
cooked.summary: SpaceX shares dropped 14% in a single day, putting the price back below its IPO launch. It turns out that when you aim for Mars, sometimes the only thing that actually hits the ground is your stock performance.
cooked.plain_english: The stock price for SpaceX fell by 14% in one day, causing its value to drop below its initial public offering price.

cooked.headline: SpaceX just had a liquidity event that would make a day trader weep
cooked.summary: The market decided that $400 billion in value was just a suggestion and deleted it in a single session. It turns out that when you go to the moon, your valuation eventually has to come back down to Earth.
cooked.plain_english: SpaceX experienced a significant decrease in its total market valuation following a recent period of growth.

cooked.headline: Your smart TV is now a side hustle for residential proxies
cooked.summary: Half the apps on your TV are turning your home internet into a public relay station without asking. It's the digital equivalent of finding out your landlord is renting out your living room while you're at work.
cooked.plain_english: Many applications on LG smart televisions include software that allows third parties to route internet traffic through the user's home network.

cooked.headline: Claude is having a collective mid-life crisis today
cooked.summary: Anthropic's models are currently throwing errors, proving that even the smartest AI needs a mental health day sometimes. It is the digital equivalent of showing up to work and immediately forgetting how to do your job.
cooked.plain_english: Claude's AI services are currently experiencing technical issues that are causing high error rates for users.
